title: Add blinding salt to scope commitment to prevent bitmask brute-force

artifacts.description: Add blindingSalt private input; replace Poseidon2(bitmask, credCommitment) with Poseidon3(bitmask, credCommitment, blindingSalt)
Same Poseidon3 change for scopeCommitment; ensure narrowed-scope delegations also carry a fresh blindingSalt
Add witness-generation tests: valid salt produces correct commitment; tampered salt fails; verify blinding hides bitmask across all ~20 valid encodings
Add tests verifying delegated scopeCommitment uses new Poseidon3 formula; scope-narrowing still enforced after salt addition
Generate cryptographically random 128-bit blindingSalt (crypto.getRandomValues) when creating agent credentials; include salt in proof inputs and CredentialBundle type
Add blindingSalt field to AgentCredential and CredentialBundle interfaces
Mirror blindingSalt field in Python AgentCredential dataclass
Update security considerations: document 128-bit minimum entropy requirement for blindingSalt, add rationale for Poseidon3 over Poseidon2, note salt must be stored alongside credential secret
Note that scope-commitment blinding mitigates OWASP LLM09 (data leakage) by preventing on-chain permission enumeration

steps: Read current AgentPolicy.circom and Delegation.circom to confirm Poseidon2 usage and existing signal names
Add `signal input blindingSalt` as a private input in AgentPolicy.circom; replace scopeCommitment component with Poseidon3(bitmask, credCommitment, blindingSalt)
Apply identical Poseidon3 change in Delegation.circom; verify scope-narrowing constraint (delegatedBitmask & parentBitmask == delegatedBitmask) remains upstream of the commitment
Recompile both circuits with `npm run compile:circuits`; confirm new constraint counts and that build artifacts are regenerated
Update sdk/src/types.ts to add blindingSalt to AgentCredential and CredentialBundle
Update sdk/src/agent.ts: generate 128-bit random salt via crypto.getRandomValues, pass it into snarkjs input object for both AgentPolicy and Delegation proofs
Update sdk-python/bolyra/types.py to mirror the blindingSalt field so the subprocess bridge forwards it correctly
Write circuit tests in agentPolicy.test.js: valid salt round-trips, wrong salt rejects, all ~20 valid cumulative-bit encodings produce distinct commitments (brute-force resistance demo)
Write circuit tests in delegation.test.js: parent and child credentials use independent salts; narrowing constraint unaffected
Update spec IETF draft security considerations section with entropy requirement and forward-secrecy note for per-session salts
Update OWASP mapping doc to reference blinding as a mitigation for on-chain scope enumeration
Run full test suite (`npm test`) and confirm no regressions
dependencies: circomlib Poseidon3 template (already available via circomlib)
pot16.ptau — re-run groth16 setup for AgentPolicy and Delegation with new constraint count
Regenerate Solidity verifier contracts from new vkey.json after trusted setup
crypto.getRandomValues available in Node 18+ (already required by SDK)
